reasoning: Rūta Meilutytė currently holds the 50m breaststroke long course world record at 29.16, set in July 2023. This is a very fast time considering the previous record was 29.30. Since the 2025 World Aquatics Championships take place in Singapore from July 27 to August 3, 2025, there is very little opportunity for the record to be broken before this major international meet. For the June dates, resolving YES is extremely likely (around 93-97%) as only national trials or minor events take place, where peak times are less common compared to the World Championships. For the August 23 and November 21 dates, the World Championships will have concluded, creating the biggest risk of the record falling. However, since the question only asks if Rūta Meilutytė will still hold the record, it will still resolve YES if she breaks her own record. Given her dominance in this specific event, the chance that *another* swimmer (such as Benedetta Pilato or Tang Qianting) breaks her WR is the only real path to NO, which is elevated after World Champs but still less likely than her retaining the record. Thus, my probability drops to ~73% after August 3 and ~68% by late November to account for further World Cup competition risks.
